ip_outcome_note: No suspected_ip flag from vision pass. Endriders are a Wizards-internal Aetherdrift faction; no external IP risk.

trivia_bullets.text: Aetherdrift (set code DFT, released 2025-02-14) is a death-race set staged across three planes — Avishkar (renamed Kaladesh), Muraganda, and the new plane Gastal — with ten competing teams in the Ghirapur Grand Prix [Scryfall API: dft set / card 4e58cb18; Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift, Part 1].
sources: Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift, Part 1

trivia_bullets.text: The Endriders are the black-red team and hail from Gastal, described by Mark Rosewater as "an apocalyptic plane of gas, draconic warlords, road warriors, and colossal dry lightning storms" [Wizards: You Get the Aetherdrift, Part 2]. MTG Wiki frames them as "nomadic apocalypse punk warriors" — directly fitting the card art's spiked-armor / junkyard-rig read [MTG Wiki: Endriders].
sources: Wizards: You Get the Aetherdrift, Part 2

trivia_bullets.text: Journalism coverage repeatedly framed Aetherdrift as "Mad Max meets Wacky Races" [Wargamer: MTG's next set features a plane we never thought we'd get to visit, 2024-11-25]. Wizards' own articles stop short of naming Mad Max directly but lean into the same vocabulary — "road warriors," "gas," "chase glory ahead and leave the dead behind" [Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift, Part 1]. The set was led in vision design by Mark Rosewater and handed to Yoni Skolnik for set design [Wizards: You Get the Aetherdrift, Part 1].
sources: Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift, Part 1
Wizards: You Get the Aetherdrift, Part 1

trivia_bullets.text: Art by Mila Pesic, a Wizards illustrator with 130+ Magic cards to her name across multiple sets [mtg.wtf artist page; Art of Magic: the Gathering]. Card has no flavor text on the printed face per Scryfall's flavor_text field (empty).
sources: Art of Magic: the Gathering: Mila Pesic

trivia_bullets.text: Mechanically a {3}{R} 3/4 Human Mercenary with Reach and Start your engines! At max speed (4), it exiles the top card of your library each upkeep, castable that turn — a red exile-impulse engine gated behind the speed mechanic [Scryfall]. EDHREC rank 18541 places it well outside Commander staple territory; commentary on the Start your engines! / Speed system has flagged it as inherently slow for a mechanic named for acceleration, with concerns about Standard viability [Game Rant: "Magic: The Gathering's Newest Mechanic May Be Dead on Arrival"; CoolStuffInc: "Mechanics of Magic: Start Your Engines/Speed"].
